response.overview: Television version aired in 1988 in two parts totaling 186 minutes. It replaced Madsen's opening monolog with a much longer description of the setting overseen by Francesca Turner that used paintings by Jaroslav Gebr. Lynch disavowed this version and had his name removed from the credits. Alan Smithee was credited, a pseudonym used by directors who wish to disavow a film. The extended and television versions additionally credit writer Lynch as Judas Booth. This version (without recap and second credit roll) has previously been released in widescreen on DVD as Dune: Extended Edition.
response.title: Dune Extended TV Edition
